docs.title: fluidize-python
docs.text: Python package for automatic generation of scientific computing software pipelines.

docs.title: ExecutionManager
docs.text:
High-level execution manager that coordinates between utilities and clients.

This is the main integration point that existing code can use to execute containers using the new universal utilities.

docs.title: _execute_docker(spec, **kwargs)
docs.text: Execute using Docker client.

docs.title: _execute_vm(spec, **kwargs)
docs.text: Execute using VM client.

docs.title: _execute_with_mode(execution_mode, spec, **kwargs)
docs.text: Execute using the appropriate client based on execution mode.

docs.title: close()
docs.text: Clean up resources.

docs.title: execute_node(project, node, prev_node=None, execution_mode=ExecutionMode.LOCAL_DOCKER, run_number=None, run_id=None, **kwargs)
docs.text:
Execute a node using the new universal utilities.

This is the main entry point that existing code can use.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default project ProjectSummary

Project information

required node nodeProperties_simulation

Node to execute

required prev_node Optional[nodeProperties_simulation]

Previous node (for dependencies)

None execution_mode ExecutionMode

How to execute the container

LOCAL_DOCKER run_number Optional[int]

Run number (optional)

None run_id Optional[str]

Run ID (optional)

None **kwargs Any

Additional execution options

{}

Returns:

Type Description dict[str, Any]

Execution result dictionary with success status and details

docs.title: PathConverter
docs.text:
Converts paths between host and container environments.

Uses existing PathFinder utility for host path resolution, then converts to appropriate container mount strategies: - Local: Clean node-specific mounts (/mnt/{node_id}) - Cloud: Bucket-wide mounts with full GCS structure

This class centralizes all environment variable construction logic, ensuring consistency across all execution methods.

docs.title: VolumeBuilder
docs.text:
Builds volume specifications for container execution.

Creates appropriate volume types and mount configurations based on execution mode: - Local: Multiple specific directory mounts (node, input, output) - Cloud: Single GCS Fuse bucket mount (input/output via env vars only)

docs.title: _build_cloud_volume_spec(context) staticmethod
docs.text:
Build volume spec for cloud execution with single GCS Fuse mount.

Cloud strategy creates only one volume: - Entire GCS bucket mount Input/output paths are accessed within this mount via environment variables

docs.title: _build_local_volume_spec(context) staticmethod
docs.text:
Build volume spec for local execution with multiple specific mounts.

Local strategy creates separate volumes for: - Node directory - Input directory (if dependencies exist) - Output directory

Graph manager for a specific project.

Provides graph operations like adding nodes/edges without requiring project context on each method call.

docs.title: GraphProcessor
docs.text:
Local filesystem-based graph processor.

Handles all graph operations using the filesystem as the source of truth, compatible with the FastAPI interface but without cloud dependencies.

docs.title: Projects
docs.text:
Manager for project CRUD operations.

Provides methods to create, retrieve, update, and list projects. All methods return Project entities that give access to project-scoped operations.

docs.title: create(project_id, label='', description='', location='', status='')
docs.text:
Create a new project.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default project_id str

Unique identifier for the project

required label str

Display label for the project

'' description str

Project description

'' location str

Project location

'' status str

Project status

''

Returns:

Type Description Project

Created project wrapped in Project class

docs.text:
Get a project by ID.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default project_id str

The project ID

required

Returns:

Type Description Project

Project wrapped in Project class

docs.text:

Type Description list[Project]

List of projects wrapped in Project class

None location Optional[str]

New status

None

Returns:

Type Description Project

Updated project wrapped in Project class

docs.title: ProjectRunner
docs.text:
Project runner that delegates to registered implementation based on mode.

Follows the same pattern as DataLoader - uses get_handler() to get the appropriate implementation (local, cloud, etc.)

docs.text: Execute a flow of nodes in the correct order nodes_to_run: List of node IDs Returns execution results for all nodes

docs.title: Fluidize Client
docs.text:
The Fluidize Client is the primary interface to create and edit projects. There are two interfaces for this, with more on the way.

  • Local Mode: Works with your local device, uses Docker to sequentially execute nodes.

  • API Mode: Runs on Fluidize API to manage projects and workflows in the cloud.

docs.title: FluidizeClient
docs.text:
High-level client for interacting with Fluidize.

This client provides an intuitive interface for managing projects, nodes, and running simulation flows. It supports two modes:

  • API mode: Connects to the Fluidize cloud API
  • Local mode: Works with local Fluidize engine installation

Configuration is handled automatically through environment variables and the FluidizeConfig class.

docs.title: mode property
docs.text: Get the current operation mode.

docs.title: FluidizeConfig
docs.text:
Lightweight configuration for fluidize library.

Handles mode switching between local and API operations, and manages paths and settings for both modes.

docs.title: is_local_mode()
docs.text: Check if running in local mode.

docs.title: is_api_mode()
docs.text: Check if running in API mode.
